checks.finding: No issues found. T-001..T-005 (high) touch sentinel surfaces per risk-classification-policy.md (access-control chokepoint; quality-gate verdict inputs; public API contract + read-only guarantee; Done-transition evidence). T-006/T-007 (medium) are recoverable installer/CI config writes. T-008 (low) is documentation-only, correctly not over-classified.

No issues found. All five Risk: high tasks include both Red->Green evidence and independent-review-verdict-recorded Done When items. No task is Risk: critical, so second-approver/signed-bundle does not apply.

T-004 (tasks.md lines 116-154) is oversized: its Scope spans four distinct implementation areas (server/index assembly, 8 core tools, 5 resources, next-command decision mapping) exceeding the more-than-three indicator, and its Done When list has 11 distinct verifiable items exceeding the more-than-eight threshold. This risks overrunning a single focused implementation session and diluting TDD/review evidence. T-001 (9 Done When items) is a secondary, less severe instance worth flagging during rework.

No issues found. Each functional task cites specific error-path AC IDs alongside happy paths (T-001 AC-003/004/017-partial; T-002 AC-002; T-003 cannot-parse fixtures; T-004 AC-012 cannot-determine + AC-017 not-found; T-005 AC-014 missing/mismatch detection; T-006 AC-008 invalid-name and AC-009 best-effort). T-008 is documentation-only and exempt.

No issues found. acceptance-tests.md test types align with scope: unit tests target single module boundaries, integration tests involve two or more components, TEST-005 e2e smoke traverses a complete MCP client workflow. All TEST rows map to a specific AC.

T-001 through T-005 (all Risk: high) contain no Rollback: field and no Done When item addressing how their changes are reverted if a defect surfaces post-merge. infra-spec.md's Rollback section documents a feature-level strategy (uninstall or git revert including dist/), but it is never referenced from within the five high-risk tasks' own sections. Absence of any in-task rollback consideration for T-001-T-005 is a gap per the ROLLBACK-PLAN check.

No issues found. Each task's Scope lists non-overlapping primary implementation files (T-001 root/path-guard/envelope; T-002 parsers/tasks.ts; T-003 five peripheral parsers; T-004 server/tools-core/resources/next-command; T-005 tools/evidence.ts; T-006 installers; T-007 workflow yml only; T-008 docs). No two tasks claim the same primary file.

tasks.md line 12 declares a strict prose execution order with genuine code dependencies (T-004 consumes T-001's path-guard/root/envelope and T-002/T-003 parsers; T-005 depends on T-003 parsers; T-007 dist-parity bundles T-001..T-005 outputs), yet every task declares Blockers: None (dependency-graph.json: 8 nodes, 0 edges). Nothing in the task-state machine prevents implement-task from starting T-004 before T-001..T-003 exist. The dependencies must be declared as Blockers so the state machine enforces order.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. All 8 tasks are feature-implementation tasks for a new MCP server.
